  • Modules
  • Data Structures
  • Files
  • Examples

Crypto API (gxcr/gxcr.h)
[gxcr User-Level Crypto Acceleration API]


Data Structures

union  gxcr_result_token_t
 The result from the Packet Processing engine. More...
struct  gxcr_context
 A context object that encapsulates data to be sent to the crypto acceleration hardware, including metadata related to the algorithm, and input and result data for the algorithm. More...

Modules

 Bulk Cipher and Digest (gxcr/tokens/bulk_crypto_digest.h)
 The Bulk Crypto Digest token is used for general-purpose cipher (encryption/decryption) and digest operations.
 HMAC Digest precalculation (gxcr/tokens/hmac_precalc.h)
 The HMAC precalculation token is used to hardware-accelerate a necessary initialization step on a context record before it can be used by the Packet Processing engine to calculate a digest in HMAC mode.
 Bulk Cipher and Digest Successive (gxcr/tokens/bulk_successive.h)
 The Bulk Successive token is used for general-purpose cipher (encryption/decryption) and digest operations when the operation cannot be completed in one transaction with the shim.

Defines

#define GXCR_AES_128_KEY_SIZE   16
 Length, in bytes, of an AES-128 key.
#define GXCR_AES_192_KEY_SIZE   24
 Length, in bytes, of an AES-192 key.
#define GXCR_AES_256_KEY_SIZE   32
 Length, in bytes, of an AES-256 key.
#define GXCR_DES_KEY_SIZE   8
 Length, in bytes, of a DES key.
#define GXCR_3DES_KEY_SIZE   24
 Length, in bytes, of a 3DES key.
#define GXCR_AES_IV_SIZE   16
 Length, in bytes, of an AES Initialization Vector.
#define GXCR_AES_GCM_IV_SIZE   12
 Length, in bytes, of an AES-GCM Initialization Vector.
#define GXCR_AES_GCM_SALT_SIZE   4
 Length, in bytes, of 4 octet 'salt' part of an AES-GCM IV.
#define GXCR_DES_IV_SIZE   8
 Length, in bytes, of a DES Initialization Vector.
#define GXCR_MD5_DIGEST_SIZE   16
 Length, in bytes, of an MD5 digest.
#define GXCR_SHA1_DIGEST_SIZE   20
 Length, in bytes, of a SHA-1 digest.
#define GXCR_SHA2_224_DIGEST_SIZE   28
 Length, in bytes, of a SHA-2 224-bit digest.
#define GXCR_SHA2_256_DIGEST_SIZE   32
 Length, in bytes, of a SHA-2 256-bit digest.
#define GXCR_SHA2_384_DIGEST_SIZE   48
 Length, in bytes, of a SHA-2 384-bit digest.
#define GXCR_SHA2_512_DIGEST_SIZE   64
 Length, in bytes, of a SHA-2 512-bit digest.
#define GXCR_AES_GCM_DIGEST_SIZE   12
 Length, in bytes, of a AES-GCM digest.
#define GXCR_ERR_PACKET_LENGTH   (1)
 Result token error code 0, Packet Length Error.
#define GXCR_ERR_TOKEN   (1 << 1)
 Result token error code 1, Unknown Token Instruction.
#define GXCR_ERR_BYPASS_DATA   (1 << 2)
 Result token error code 2, Too Much Bypass Data.
#define GXCR_ERR_CRYPTO_BLOCK_SIZE   (1 << 3)
 Result token error code 3, Crypto Block Size Error.
#define GXCR_ERR_HASH_BLOCK_SIZE   (1 << 4)
 Result token error code 4, Hash Block Size Error.
#define GXCR_ERR_INVALID_COMBO   (1 << 5)
 Result token error code 5, Invalid Algorithm/mode Combination.
#define GXCR_ERR_PROHIBITED_ALGO   (1 << 6)
 Result token error code 6, Prohibited Algorithm.
#define GXCR_ERR_HASH_INPUT_OVERFLOW   (1 << 7)
 Result token error code 7, Hash Input Overflow.
#define GXCR_ERR_TTL_UNDERFLOW   (1 << 8)
 Result token error code 8, TTL/HOP-limit Underflow.
#define GXCR_ERR_AUTHENTICATION   (1 << 9)
 Result token error code 9, Authentication Failed.
#define GXCR_ERR_SEQNUM_CHECK   (1 << 10)
 Result token error code 10, Sequence Number Check Failed.
#define GXCR_ERR_SPI_CHECK   (1 << 11)
 Result token error code 11, SPI Check Failed.
#define GXCR_ERR_CHECKSUM   (1 << 12)
 Result token error code 12, Checksum Incorrect.
#define GXCR_ERR_PAD_VERIFICATION   (1 << 13)
 Result token error code 13, Pad Verification Failed.
#define GXCR_ERR_TIMEOUT   (1 << 14)
 Result token error code 14, Timeout Error.
#define GXCR_ERR_OUTPUT_DMA   (1)
 Result token error code 15, Output DMA Error.

Typedefs

typedef struct gxcr_context gxcr_context_t
 A context object that encapsulates data to be sent to the crypto acceleration hardware, including metadata related to the algorithm, and input and result data for the algorithm.

Enumerations

enum  gxcr_cipher_t {
  GXCR_CIPHER_NONE, GXCR_CIPHER_AES_CBC_128, GXCR_CIPHER_AES_CBC_192, GXCR_CIPHER_AES_CBC_256,
  GXCR_CIPHER_AES_ECB_128, GXCR_CIPHER_AES_ECB_192, GXCR_CIPHER_AES_ECB_256, GXCR_CIPHER_AES_ICM_128,
  GXCR_CIPHER_AES_ICM_192, GXCR_CIPHER_AES_ICM_256, GXCR_CIPHER_AES_CCM, GXCR_CIPHER_AES_GCM_128,
  GXCR_CIPHER_AES_GCM_192, GXCR_CIPHER_AES_GCM_256, GXCR_CIPHER_DES_ECB, GXCR_CIPHER_DES_CBC,
  GXCR_CIPHER_3DES_ECB, GXCR_CIPHER_3DES_CBC, GXCR_CIPHER_NONE_NOCOPY, GXCR_CIPHER_NUM_CIPHERS
}
 Enumeration for available ciphers. More...
enum  gxcr_digest_t {
  GXCR_DIGEST_NONE, GXCR_DIGEST_MD5, GXCR_DIGEST_SHA1, GXCR_DIGEST_SHA_224,
  GXCR_DIGEST_SHA_256, GXCR_DIGEST_SHA_384, GXCR_DIGEST_SHA_512, GXCR_DIGEST_AES_XCBC_MAC_96,
  GXCR_DIGEST_AES_GCM, GXCR_DIGEST_NUM_DIGESTS
}
 Enumeration for available digest algorithms. More...
enum  gxcr_digest_stage_t { GXCR_DIGEST_STAGE_INIT = 3, GXCR_DIGEST_STAGE_UPDATE = 2, GXCR_DIGEST_STAGE_FINAL = 0 }
 Enumeration indicating which hash stage to perform in a multi-stage digest calculation. More...

Functions

size_t gxcr_calc_context_bytes (gxcr_cipher_t cipher, gxcr_digest_t digest, int hmac_mode, int successive)
 Function to find out how much memory to allocate for a particular crypto_context.
int gxcr_init_context (gxcr_context_t *crypto_context, void *metadata_mem, int metadata_mem_len, gxcr_cipher_t cipher, gxcr_digest_t digest, unsigned char *key, unsigned char *iv, int encrypt, int cipher_then_digest, int hmac_mode)
 Make a gxcr_context_t for a particular cipher/digest in the given memory.
int gxcr_init_context_successive_crypto (gxcr_context_t *crypto_context, void *metadata_mem, int metadata_mem_len, gxcr_cipher_t cipher, unsigned char *key, unsigned char *iv, int encrypt)
 Make a gxcr_context_t intended for use with successive operations (as when the entire data buffer cannot be processed in one operation) for a particular cipher in the given memory.
int gxcr_init_context_successive_digest (gxcr_context_t *crypto_context, void *metadata_mem, int metadata_mem_len, gxcr_digest_t digest)
 Make a gxcr_context_t intended for use with successive operations (as when the entire data buffer cannot be processed in one operation) for a particular digest in the given memory.
size_t hmac_precalc_calc_metadata_bytes (int digest_len)
 Function to find out how much metadata memory to allocate for use by gxcr_hmac_precalc for the purposes of performing the necessary hmac key precalculation step with gxcr_hmac_precalc().
int gxcr_hmac_precalc (gxio_mica_context_t *mica_context, gxcr_context_t *crypto_context, void *scratch_mem, int scratch_mem_len, void *hmac_key, int hmac_key_len)
 This function uses the MiCA accelerator to perform further initialization of the gxcr_context_t data structure, when HMAC mode is used for the digest.
int gxcr_do_op (gxio_mica_context_t *mica_context, gxcr_context_t *crypto_context, void *src, void *dst, int len)
 Run the operation indicated by the initialized gxcr_context.
int gxcr_start_op (gxio_mica_context_t *mica_context, gxcr_context_t *crypto_context, void *src, void *dst, int len)
 Start the operation indicated by the initialized gxcr_context.
void gxcr_wait_for_completion (gxio_mica_context_t *mica_context)
 Block until the operation that has been started on a mica_context by a non-blocking function has completed.
int gxcr_do_init_op (gxio_mica_context_t *mica_context, gxcr_context_t *crypto_context, void *src, void *dst, int len)
 Run the first operation of a successive operation indicated by the initialized gxcr_context.
int gxcr_do_update_op (gxio_mica_context_t *mica_context, gxcr_context_t *crypto_context, void *src, void *dst, int len)
 Run the middle operations of a successive operation indicated by the initialized gxcr_context.
int gxcr_do_final_op (gxio_mica_context_t *mica_context, gxcr_context_t *crypto_context, void *src, void *dst, int len)
 Run the last operation of a successive operation indicated by the initialized gxcr_context.
void * gxcr_context_key (gxcr_context_t *crypto_context)
 Returns a pointer to the cipher key data for a gxcr_context_t, NULL if this gxcr_context_t doesn't do a cipher.
int gxcr_context_key_len (gxcr_context_t *crypto_context)
 Returns the cipher key length for a gxcr_context_t, 0 if this gxcr_context_t doesn't do a cipher.
void * gxcr_context_iv (gxcr_context_t *crypto_context)
 Returns a pointer to the initialization vector data for a gxcr_context_t, NULL if this gxcr_context_t doesn't do a cipher.
int gxcr_context_iv_len (gxcr_context_t *crypto_context)
 Returns the length of the initialization vector for a gxcr_context_t, 0 if this gxcr_context_t doesn't do a cipher.
void * gxcr_context_digest0 (gxcr_context_t *crypto_context)
 Returns a pointer to the calculated digest data for a gxcr_context_t, NULL if this gxcr_context_t doesn't do a hash.
int gxcr_context_digest0_len (gxcr_context_t *crypto_context)
 Returns the length of the digest for a gxcr_context_t, 0 if this gxcr_context_t doesn't do a hash.
void * gxcr_context_digest1 (gxcr_context_t *crypto_context)
 Returns a pointer to the calculated digest data for a gxcr_context_t, NULL if this gxcr_context_t doesn't do a hash.
int gxcr_context_digest1_len (gxcr_context_t *crypto_context)
 Returns the length of the digest for a gxcr_context_t, 0 if this gxcr_context_t doesn't do a hash.
uint32_t gxcr_result_packet_length (gxcr_result_token_t *res)
 Function to provide endian-agnostic access to the packet length field of the little-endian result token.
uint32_t gxcr_result_error (gxcr_result_token_t *res)
 Function to provide endian-agnostic access to the error field of the little-endian result token.
const char * gxcr_strerror (int error)
 Function to convert a GXCR error code to a string.

Define Documentation

#define GXCR_AES_128_KEY_SIZE   16

Length, in bytes, of an AES-128 key.

Examples:
mica/crypto/app.c, mica/ipsec/app.c, and mica/queue/app.c.

#define GXCR_AES_192_KEY_SIZE   24

Length, in bytes, of an AES-192 key.

#define GXCR_AES_256_KEY_SIZE   32

Length, in bytes, of an AES-256 key.

#define GXCR_DES_KEY_SIZE   8

Length, in bytes, of a DES key.

#define GXCR_3DES_KEY_SIZE   24

Length, in bytes, of a 3DES key.

#define GXCR_AES_IV_SIZE   16

Length, in bytes, of an AES Initialization Vector.

Examples:
mica/ipsec/app.c, and mica/queue/app.c.

#define GXCR_AES_GCM_IV_SIZE   12

Length, in bytes, of an AES-GCM Initialization Vector.

#define GXCR_AES_GCM_SALT_SIZE   4

Length, in bytes, of 4 octet 'salt' part of an AES-GCM IV.

#define GXCR_DES_IV_SIZE   8

Length, in bytes, of a DES Initialization Vector.

#define GXCR_MD5_DIGEST_SIZE   16

Length, in bytes, of an MD5 digest.

Examples:
mica/crypto/app.c, mica/ipsec/app.c, and mica/queue/app.c.

#define GXCR_SHA1_DIGEST_SIZE   20

Length, in bytes, of a SHA-1 digest.

#define GXCR_SHA2_224_DIGEST_SIZE   28

Length, in bytes, of a SHA-2 224-bit digest.

#define GXCR_SHA2_256_DIGEST_SIZE   32

Length, in bytes, of a SHA-2 256-bit digest.

#define GXCR_SHA2_384_DIGEST_SIZE   48

Length, in bytes, of a SHA-2 384-bit digest.

#define GXCR_SHA2_512_DIGEST_SIZE   64

Length, in bytes, of a SHA-2 512-bit digest.

#define GXCR_AES_GCM_DIGEST_SIZE   12

Length, in bytes, of a AES-GCM digest.

#define GXCR_ERR_PACKET_LENGTH   (1)

Result token error code 0, Packet Length Error.

#define GXCR_ERR_TOKEN   (1 << 1)

Result token error code 1, Unknown Token Instruction.

#define GXCR_ERR_BYPASS_DATA   (1 << 2)

Result token error code 2, Too Much Bypass Data.

#define GXCR_ERR_CRYPTO_BLOCK_SIZE   (1 << 3)

Result token error code 3, Crypto Block Size Error.

#define GXCR_ERR_HASH_BLOCK_SIZE   (1 << 4)

Result token error code 4, Hash Block Size Error.

#define GXCR_ERR_INVALID_COMBO   (1 << 5)

Result token error code 5, Invalid Algorithm/mode Combination.

#define GXCR_ERR_PROHIBITED_ALGO   (1 << 6)

Result token error code 6, Prohibited Algorithm.

#define GXCR_ERR_HASH_INPUT_OVERFLOW   (1 << 7)

Result token error code 7, Hash Input Overflow.

#define GXCR_ERR_TTL_UNDERFLOW   (1 << 8)

Result token error code 8, TTL/HOP-limit Underflow.

#define GXCR_ERR_AUTHENTICATION   (1 << 9)

Result token error code 9, Authentication Failed.

#define GXCR_ERR_SEQNUM_CHECK   (1 << 10)

Result token error code 10, Sequence Number Check Failed.

#define GXCR_ERR_SPI_CHECK   (1 << 11)

Result token error code 11, SPI Check Failed.

#define GXCR_ERR_CHECKSUM   (1 << 12)

Result token error code 12, Checksum Incorrect.

#define GXCR_ERR_PAD_VERIFICATION   (1 << 13)

Result token error code 13, Pad Verification Failed.

#define GXCR_ERR_TIMEOUT   (1 << 14)

Result token error code 14, Timeout Error.

#define GXCR_ERR_OUTPUT_DMA   (1)

Result token error code 15, Output DMA Error.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct gxcr_context gxcr_context_t

A context object that encapsulates data to be sent to the crypto acceleration hardware, including metadata related to the algorithm, and input and result data for the algorithm.

The internals of this undocumented structure are not for direct use by users.

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum gxcr_cipher_t

Enumeration for available ciphers.

Enumerator:
GXCR_CIPHER_NONE  No cipher, copy source data to destination buffer.
GXCR_CIPHER_AES_CBC_128  AES-CBC with 128-bit key.

GXCR_CIPHER_AES_CBC_192  AES-CBC with 192-bit key.

GXCR_CIPHER_AES_CBC_256  AES-CBC with 256-bit key.

GXCR_CIPHER_AES_ECB_128  AES-ECB with 128-bit key.

GXCR_CIPHER_AES_ECB_192  AES-ECB with 192-bit key.

GXCR_CIPHER_AES_ECB_256  AES-ECB with 256-bit key.

GXCR_CIPHER_AES_ICM_128  AES-ICM with 128-bit key.

GXCR_CIPHER_AES_ICM_192  AES-ICM with 192-bit key.

GXCR_CIPHER_AES_ICM_256  AES-ICM with 256-bit key.

GXCR_CIPHER_AES_CCM  AES-CCM.
GXCR_CIPHER_AES_GCM_128  AES-GCM with 128-bit key.
GXCR_CIPHER_AES_GCM_192  AES-GCM with 192-bit key.
GXCR_CIPHER_AES_GCM_256  AES-GCM with 256-bit key.
GXCR_CIPHER_DES_ECB  DES-ECB.
GXCR_CIPHER_DES_CBC  DES-CBC.
GXCR_CIPHER_3DES_ECB  3DES-ECB
GXCR_CIPHER_3DES_CBC  3DES-CBC
GXCR_CIPHER_NONE_NOCOPY  No cipher, do not copy the source data to the destination buffer.

GXCR_CIPHER_NUM_CIPHERS  Number of supported cipher algorithms.

enum gxcr_digest_t

Enumeration for available digest algorithms.

Enumerator:
GXCR_DIGEST_NONE  No digest.

GXCR_DIGEST_MD5  MD5 digest.

GXCR_DIGEST_SHA1  SHA-1 digest.

GXCR_DIGEST_SHA_224  SHA-2 224-bit digest.

GXCR_DIGEST_SHA_256  SHA-2 256-bit digest.

GXCR_DIGEST_SHA_384  SHA-2 384-bit digest.

GXCR_DIGEST_SHA_512  SHA-2 512-bit digest.

GXCR_DIGEST_AES_XCBC_MAC_96  XCBC digest.

GXCR_DIGEST_AES_GCM  AES-GCM digest.
GXCR_DIGEST_NUM_DIGESTS  Number of supported digest algorithms.

enum gxcr_digest_stage_t

Enumeration indicating which hash stage to perform in a multi-stage digest calculation.

Enumerator:
GXCR_DIGEST_STAGE_INIT  Initial stage, must be first in a multi-stage digest.

GXCR_DIGEST_STAGE_UPDATE  Update stage, must be after init stage in a multi-stage digest.

GXCR_DIGEST_STAGE_FINAL  Final stage, must be either the only stage, or the last stage in a multi-stage digest.

Function Documentation

size_t gxcr_calc_context_bytes ( gxcr_cipher_t  cipher,
gxcr_digest_t  digest,
int  hmac_mode,
int  successive 
)

Function to find out how much memory to allocate for a particular crypto_context.

Parameters:
cipher Cipher algorithm to be used by operations performed with the crypto_context.
digest Digest algorithm to be used by operations performed with the crypto_context.
hmac_mode 1 if the digest will use HMAC, 0 if not.
successive 1 if the crypto_context will be used for successive operations, 0 if not.

int gxcr_init_context ( gxcr_context_t *  crypto_context,
void *  metadata_mem,
int  metadata_mem_len,
gxcr_cipher_t  cipher,
gxcr_digest_t  digest,
unsigned char *  key,
unsigned char *  iv,
int  encrypt,
int  cipher_then_digest,
int  hmac_mode 
)

Make a gxcr_context_t for a particular cipher/digest in the given memory.

Metadata memory must be registered via gxio_mica_register_page(). This function will look up the metadata information and initialize the gxcr_context with information on valid fields and field sizes.

Parameters:
crypto_context Pointer to the context to be initialized.
metadata_mem Pointer to registered I/O memory to use for this context.
metadata_mem_len Length of provided memory.
cipher Which cipher algorithm to use.
digest Which digest algorithm to use.
key Encryption key (NULL if no cipher).
iv Initialization vector (NULL for 0 iv, or if no cipher).
encrypt 1 if cipher should encrypt, 0 for decrypt.
cipher_then_digest 1 if cipher should be run before digest, 0 if digest should be calculated before the cipher algorithm is run.
hmac_mode 1 if the digest will use HMAC, 0 if not.
Returns:
GXCR_NO_ERROR on success, GXCR_OPERATION_NOT_SUPPORTED if the requested set of algorithms is not supported.
Examples:
mica/crypto/app.c.

int gxcr_init_context_successive_crypto ( gxcr_context_t *  crypto_context,
void *  metadata_mem,
int  metadata_mem_len,
gxcr_cipher_t  cipher,
unsigned char *  key,
unsigned char *  iv,
int  encrypt 
)

Make a gxcr_context_t intended for use with successive operations (as when the entire data buffer cannot be processed in one operation) for a particular cipher in the given memory.

Metadata memory must be registered via gxio_mica_register_page().

Parameters:
crypto_context Pointer to the context to be initialized.
metadata_mem Pointer to registered I/O memory to use for this context.
metadata_mem_len Length of provided memory.
cipher Which cipher algorithm to use.
key Encryption key (NULL if no cipher).
iv Initialization vector (NULL for 0 iv, or if no cipher).
encrypt 1 if cipher should encrypt, 0 for decrypt.
Returns:
GXCR_NO_ERROR on success, error code on failure.

int gxcr_init_context_successive_digest ( gxcr_context_t *  crypto_context,
void *  metadata_mem,
int  metadata_mem_len,
gxcr_digest_t  digest 
)

Make a gxcr_context_t intended for use with successive operations (as when the entire data buffer cannot be processed in one operation) for a particular digest in the given memory.

Metadata memory must be registered via gxio_mica_register_page().

Parameters:
crypto_context Pointer to the context to be initialized.
metadata_mem Pointer to registered I/O memory to use for this context.
metadata_mem_len Length of provided memory.
digest Which digest algorithm to use.
Returns:
GXCR_NO_ERROR on success, error code on failure.

size_t hmac_precalc_calc_metadata_bytes ( int  digest_len  ) 

Function to find out how much metadata memory to allocate for use by gxcr_hmac_precalc for the purposes of performing the necessary hmac key precalculation step with gxcr_hmac_precalc().

Parameters:
digest_len Length of the digest field for this algorithm.

int gxcr_hmac_precalc ( gxio_mica_context_t *  mica_context,
gxcr_context_t *  crypto_context,
void *  scratch_mem,
int  scratch_mem_len,
void *  hmac_key,
int  hmac_key_len 
)

This function uses the MiCA accelerator to perform further initialization of the gxcr_context_t data structure, when HMAC mode is used for the digest.

This function must be run before the gxcr_context_t is used for a digest operation if HMAC mode is used. It must not be run if HMAC mode is not used.

Parameters:
mica_context An initialized MiCA context.
crypto_context An initialized gxcr context.
scratch_mem Pointer to scratch memory to be used by the shim. Memory must be 32-bit aligned and registered with the MiCA shim via gxio_mica_register_page().
scratch_mem_len Length of provided precalc metadata memory, in bytes. The value should be at least max(hmac_key_len, data block size) + metadata size calculated by function: hmac_precalc_calc_metadata_bytes(.).
hmac_key Pointer to the HMAC key.
hmac_key_len The length, in bytes, of the HMAC key.
Returns:
GXCR_NO_ERROR on success, an error code otherwise.

int gxcr_do_op ( gxio_mica_context_t *  mica_context,
gxcr_context_t *  crypto_context,
void *  src,
void *  dst,
int  len 
)

Run the operation indicated by the initialized gxcr_context.

This function blocks until the shim has completed the operation.

Parameters:
mica_context An initialized MiCA context.
crypto_context An initialized gxcr context.
src Pointer to source data, memory must be registered with the MiCA shim via gxio_mica_register_page().
dst Pointer to destination memory, memory must be registered with the MiCA shim via gxio_mica_register_page().
len Length, in bytes, of the data to be processed.
Examples:
mica/crypto/app.c.

int gxcr_start_op ( gxio_mica_context_t *  mica_context,
gxcr_context_t *  crypto_context,
void *  src,
void *  dst,
int  len 
)

Start the operation indicated by the initialized gxcr_context.

This function returns immediately. The function gxio_mica_is_busy() may be called to poll for completion, or gxcr_wait_for_completion() to block until completion.

Parameters:
mica_context An initialized MiCA context.
crypto_context An initialized gxcr context.
src Pointer to source data, memory must be registered with the MiCA shim via gxio_mica_register_page().
dst Pointer to destination memory, memory must be registered with the MiCA shim via gxio_mica_register_page().
len Length, in bytes, of the data to be processed.

void gxcr_wait_for_completion ( gxio_mica_context_t *  mica_context  ) 

Block until the operation that has been started on a mica_context by a non-blocking function has completed.

Parameters:
mica_context An initialized MiCA context.

int gxcr_do_init_op ( gxio_mica_context_t *  mica_context,
gxcr_context_t *  crypto_context,
void *  src,
void *  dst,
int  len 
)

Run the first operation of a successive operation indicated by the initialized gxcr_context.

This function blocks until the shim has completed the operation.

Parameters:
mica_context An initialized MiCA context.
crypto_context An initialized gxcr context.
src Pointer to source data, memory must be registered with the MiCA shim via gxio_mica_register_page().
dst Pointer to destination memory, memory must be registered with the MiCA shim via gxio_mica_register_page().
len Length, in bytes, of the data to be processed.

int gxcr_do_update_op ( gxio_mica_context_t *  mica_context,
gxcr_context_t *  crypto_context,
void *  src,
void *  dst,
int  len 
)

Run the middle operations of a successive operation indicated by the initialized gxcr_context.

This function blocks until the shim has completed the operation.

Parameters:
mica_context An initialized MiCA context.
crypto_context An initialized gxcr context.
src Pointer to source data, memory must be registered with the MiCA shim via gxio_mica_register_page().
dst Pointer to destination memory, memory must be registered with the MiCA shim via gxio_mica_register_page().
len Length, in bytes, of the data to be processed.

int gxcr_do_final_op ( gxio_mica_context_t *  mica_context,
gxcr_context_t *  crypto_context,
void *  src,
void *  dst,
int  len 
)

Run the last operation of a successive operation indicated by the initialized gxcr_context.

This function blocks until the shim has completed the operation.

Parameters:
mica_context An initialized MiCA context.
crypto_context An initialized gxcr context.
src Pointer to source data, memory must be registered with the MiCA shim via gxio_mica_register_page().
dst Pointer to destination memory, memory must be registered with the MiCA shim via gxio_mica_register_page().
len Length, in bytes, of the data to be processed.

void* gxcr_context_key ( gxcr_context_t *  crypto_context  ) 

Returns a pointer to the cipher key data for a gxcr_context_t, NULL if this gxcr_context_t doesn't do a cipher.

Parameters:
crypto_context An initialized gxcr_context.

int gxcr_context_key_len ( gxcr_context_t *  crypto_context  ) 

Returns the cipher key length for a gxcr_context_t, 0 if this gxcr_context_t doesn't do a cipher.

Parameters:
crypto_context An initialized gxcr_context.

void* gxcr_context_iv ( gxcr_context_t *  crypto_context  ) 

Returns a pointer to the initialization vector data for a gxcr_context_t, NULL if this gxcr_context_t doesn't do a cipher.

Parameters:
crypto_context An initialized gxcr_context.

int gxcr_context_iv_len ( gxcr_context_t *  crypto_context  ) 

Returns the length of the initialization vector for a gxcr_context_t, 0 if this gxcr_context_t doesn't do a cipher.

Parameters:
crypto_context An initialized gxcr_context.

void* gxcr_context_digest0 ( gxcr_context_t *  crypto_context  ) 

Returns a pointer to the calculated digest data for a gxcr_context_t, NULL if this gxcr_context_t doesn't do a hash.

Parameters:
crypto_context An initialized gxcr_context.

int gxcr_context_digest0_len ( gxcr_context_t *  crypto_context  ) 

Returns the length of the digest for a gxcr_context_t, 0 if this gxcr_context_t doesn't do a hash.

Parameters:
crypto_context An initialized gxcr_context.

void* gxcr_context_digest1 ( gxcr_context_t *  crypto_context  ) 

Returns a pointer to the calculated digest data for a gxcr_context_t, NULL if this gxcr_context_t doesn't do a hash.

Parameters:
crypto_context An initialized gxcr_context.

int gxcr_context_digest1_len ( gxcr_context_t *  crypto_context  ) 

Returns the length of the digest for a gxcr_context_t, 0 if this gxcr_context_t doesn't do a hash.

Parameters:
crypto_context An initialized gxcr_context.

uint32_t gxcr_result_packet_length ( gxcr_result_token_t *  res  ) 

Function to provide endian-agnostic access to the packet length field of the little-endian result token.

Parameters:
res Pointer to result token in metadata memory.
Examples:
mica/ipsec/app.c.

uint32_t gxcr_result_error ( gxcr_result_token_t *  res  ) 

Function to provide endian-agnostic access to the error field of the little-endian result token.

Parameters:
res Pointer to result token in metadata memory.
Examples:
mica/ipsec/app.c.

const char* gxcr_strerror ( int  error  ) 

Function to convert a GXCR error code to a string.

Parameters:
error An error code.
Returns:
An error string.
